title: Madhuca selangorica
taxonomy: Madhuca selangorica is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Malaysia and is commonly found in the wild. The classification of Madhuca selangorica includes being part of the genus Madhuca and the family Sapotaceae. The species is a type of tree that grows in tropical regions. It belongs to the order Ericales and is a member of the clade Angiosperms.
uses: Madhuca selangorica has been traditionally used in various medicinal practices, although its effectiveness and safety may not be extensively documented. Its bark, seeds, and leaves are reported to possess certain medicinal properties. Traditionally, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Madhuca selangorica, also known as the Selangor mahua tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. This plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Madhuca selangorica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory issues and skin irritations.
